content.thinking: The producer is failing because it doesn't have permission to declare a queue in the /app vhost. Let me check what vhosts exist, what users exist, and what permissions they have.

content.text: The producer lacks permissions on the `/app` vhost. Let me gather more details.

content.content:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  annotations:
    deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: "1"
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"apps/v1","kind":"Deployment","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"app-producer","namespace":"karma-rabbitmq-security-upgrade-v2-01-5ecd4369-cluster-a"},"spec":{"replicas":1,"selector":{"matchLabels":{"app":"app-producer"}},"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"app-producer"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["/bin/sh","-c","set -e\nbase=\"http://rabbitmq:15672/api\"\nvhost=\"%2Fapp\"\nqueue=\"app-queue\"\nauth=\"${APP_USER}:${APP_PASS}\"\nfor i in $(seq 1 60); do\n  if curl -s -u \"$auth\" \"$base/vhosts\" \u003e/dev/null; then\n    break\n  fi\n  sleep 2\ndone\npayload='{\"durable\":true,\"auto_delete\":false,\"arguments\":{\"x-queue-type\":\"classic\",\"x-queue-mode\":\"lazy\"}}'\ncode=$(curl -s -o /tmp/resp -w \"%{http_code}\" -u \"$auth\" \\\n  -H \"content-type: application/json\" \\\n  -XPUT \"$base/queues/${vhost}/${queue}\" -d \"$payload\")\nif [ \"$code\" -ge 300 ]; then\n  echo \"app-producer: failed to declare queue in vhost /app: $(cat /tmp/resp)\"\n  exit 1\nfi\ni=0\nwhile true; do\n  body='{\"properties\":{},\"routing_key\":\"'\"${queue}\"'\",\"payload\":\"msg-'\"${i}\"'\",\"payload_encoding\":\"string\"}'\n  code=$(curl -s -o /tmp/pubresp -w \"%{http_code}\" -u \"$auth\" \\\n    -H \"content-type: application/json\" \\\n    -XPOST \"$base/exchanges/${vhost}/amq.default/publish\" -d \"$body\")\n  if [ \"$code\" -ne 200 ]; then\n    echo \"app-producer: publish failed: $(cat /tmp/pubresp)\"\n    exit 1\n  fi\n  i=$((i+1))\n  sleep 2\ndone\n"],"env":[{"name":"APP_USER","valueFrom":{"secretKeyRef":{"key":"username","name":"rabbitmq-app"}}},{"name":"APP_PASS","valueFrom":{"secretKeyRef":{"key":"password","name":"rabbitmq-app"}}}],"image":"curlimages/curl:8.6.0","name":"producer"}]}}}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-07-01T22:12:50Z"
  generation: 1
  name: app-producer
  namespace: karma-rabbitmq-security-upgrade-v2-01-5ecd4369-cluster-a
  resourceVersion: "1873964"
  uid: f0d8d80e-6dee-4f2d-9d94-e41e493be905
spec:
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 600
  replicas: 1
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: app-producer
  strategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 25%
      maxUnavailable: 25%
    type: RollingUpdate
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: app-producer
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - /bin/sh
        - -c
        - |
          set -e
          base="http://rabbitmq:15672/api"
          vhost="%2Fapp"
          queue="app-queue"
          auth="${APP_USER}:${APP_PASS}"
          for i in $(seq 1 60); do
            if curl -s -u "$auth" "$base/vhosts" >/dev/null; then
              break
            fi
            sleep 2
          done
          payload='{"durable":true,"auto_delete":false,"arguments":{"x-queue-type":"classic","x-queue-mode":"lazy"}}'
          code=$(curl -s -o /tmp/resp -w "%{http_code}" -u "$auth" \
            -H "content-type: application/json" \
            -XPUT "$base/queues/${vhost}/${queue}" -d "$payload")
          if [ "$code" -ge 300 ]; then
            echo "app-producer: failed to declare queue in vhost /app: $(cat /tmp/resp)"
            exit 1
          fi
          i=0
          while true; do
            body='{"properties":{},"routing_key":"'"${queue}"'","payload":"msg-'"${i}"'","payload_encoding":"string"}'
            code=$(curl -s -o /tmp/pubresp -w "%{http_code}" -u "$auth" \
              -H "content-type: application/json" \
              -XPOST "$base/exchanges/${vhost}/amq.default/publish" -d "$body")
            if [ "$code" -ne 200 ]; then
              echo "app-producer: publish failed: $(cat /tmp/pubresp)"
              exit 1
            fi
            i=$((i+1))
            sleep 2
          done
        env:
        - name: APP_USER
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              key: username
              name: rabbitmq-app
        - name: APP_PASS
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              key: password
              name: rabbitmq-app
        image: curlimages/curl:8.6.0
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: producer
        resources: {}
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
status:
  availableReplicas: 1
  conditions:
  - lastTransitionTime: "2026-07-01T22:12:50Z"
    lastUpdateTime: "2026-07-01T22:12:54Z"
    message: ReplicaSet "app-producer-57bb4c6f8d" has successfully progressed.
    reason: NewReplicaSetAvailable
    status: "True"
    type: Progressing
  - lastTransitionTime: "2026-07-01T22:13:11Z"
    lastUpdateTime: "2026-07-01T22:13:11Z"
    message: Deployment has minimum availability.
    reason: MinimumReplicasAvailable
    status: "True"
    type: Available
  observedGeneration: 1
  readyReplicas: 1
  replicas: 1
  updatedReplicas: 1

content.thinking:
Now I can see the issue clearly:
1. The producer uses credentials from the `rabbitmq-app` secret (username/password)
2. The user is `app-user` (I need to verify this from the secret)
3. The `/app` vhost exists but `app-user` lacks permissions on it

Let me check what's in the secret and what permissions the user currently has on the `/app` vhost.

content.text: Now I can see the producer uses the `rabbitmq-app` secret. Let me check the secret contents and current permissions.

input.command: kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-security-upgrade-v2-01-5ecd4369-cluster-a get secret rabbitmq-app -o jsonpath='{.data.username}' | base64 -d && echo && kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-security-upgrade-v2-01-5ecd4369-cluster-a get secret rabbitmq-app -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d && echo
